title: Session Handoff Badge
prompt:
Create a production-ready Session Handoff Badge using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original status control lets a person set a short work session to Ready for handoff or Keep private. The badge uses a cyan dot that travels between two named labels, while a concise message explains what the current setting means. The copy must explicitly say the active state, so neither color nor position is the only signal. Use a light Bento composition with #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, a soft neutral shadow, #111111 primary type, and #4A4A4F support copy. Cyan is the single bright accent and no card or badge surface may use a gradient.

Use one native button with role switch, aria-checked, a 44px minimum touch target, explicit accessible name, and a polite aria-live status. Click, Enter, and Space toggle safely. Move the dot with a spring using stiffness 360 and damping 28, fade the message over 150ms ease-out, and use a 90ms press scale of 0.98. Keep all state local and deterministic: no timers, random values, browser storage, remote assets, network requests, layout reads, or external side effects. Favor transform and opacity.

At 320px, allow message text to wrap without clipping the control; at a detail preview, permit a comfortable maximum width. Include visible 3px focus rings, logical order, AA contrast, and both written and shape state cues. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the dot and retaining only a short opacity fade. Return complete paste-ready client code, runnable pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [r,setR]=React.useState(false);return
SESSION SHARING
{r?'Ready for handoff':'Keep private'}

{r?'A teammate can continue this session.':'Only you can continue this session.'}

Session is {r?'ready for handoff':'kept private'}.

}return ;})()
